It develops advanced skills in GIS, cartography, and geospatial data technologies alongside a six-month, credit-bearing professional placement. Replacing the traditional MSc thesis, the placement enables students to apply technical expertise directly within industry, government, or consultancy environments. Graduates complete the programme with practical experience, professional confidence, and strong employability in the geospatial sector. programme.name: MSc Geoinformation Technology & Cartography with Work Placement

items.reviewReason: At least one opening date is not published as an exact date; confirm it on the programme page.
items.evidenceExcerpt: Application deadlines search Study Research Explore Connect Search icon Close menu icon Menu icon bar 1 Menu icon bar 2 Menu icon bar 3 Home Postgraduate study Taught degree programmes A‑Z Geoinformation Technology & Cartography with Work Placement Postgraduate study Postgraduate study Taught degree programmes A‑Z Geoinformation Technology & Cartography with Work Placement How to apply Eng lang ERs Application deadlines Postgraduate taught Geoinformation Technology & Cartography with Work Placement MSc Our Masters in Geoinformation Technology & Cartography with Work Placement is an industry-focused programme. It develops advanced skills in GIS, cartography, and geospatial data technologies alongside a six-month, credit-bearing professional placement. Replacing the traditional MSc thesis, the placement enables students to apply technical expertise directly within industry, government, or consultancy environments. Graduates complete the programme with practical experience, professional confidence, and strong employability in the geospatial sector. You will receive our decision on your application by 16 January 2026. Round 2 application dates: 6 November 2025 to 17 December 2025 All International and EU applications submitted within these dates will be reviewed with no priority given to any geographic region. You will receive our decision on your application by 25 February 2026. Round 3 application dates: 18 December 2025 to 4 February 2026 Priority will be given to under-represented geographic regions. Round 1 application dates: 1 October 2025 to 17 November 2025 All applications submitted within these dates will be reviewed with no priority given to any geographic region. You will receive our decision on your application by 29 January 2026. Round 2 application dates: 18 November 2025 to 16 February 2026 All applications submitted within these dates will be reviewed with no priority given to any geographic region. You will receive our decision on your application by 24 March 2026. Round 3 application dates: 17 February 2026 to 25 May 2026 Priority will be given to under-represented geographic regions. You will receive our decision on your application by 6 July 2026. Round 4 application dates: 26 May 2026 to 1 July 2026 Priority will be given to under-represented geographic regions.
